metadata.description: Hardcoded clinically validated drug-drug interactions used when LLM is unavailable.

interactions.mechanism: Warfarin inhibits clotting factor synthesis (vitamin K antagonist); aspirin inhibits platelet aggregation via COX-1 and adds anticoagulant effect. Additive bleeding risk.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid concurrent use unless clinically indicated (e.g., mechanical heart valve). If necessary, use lowest effective aspirin dose (75 mg) and monitor INR closely.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit COX-1 reducing platelet aggregation and gastric mucosal protection. May displace warfarin from protein binding, raising free warfarin levels.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid. Use paracetamol (acetaminophen) for analgesia instead. Monitor INR closely if NSAID use is unavoidable.

interactions.mechanism: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9, the primary metabolic pathway for S-warfarin, substantially raising warfarin plasma levels and anticoagulant effect.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose by ~25–50% when initiating fluconazole. Monitor INR every 2–3 days until stable.

interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits CYP3A4 and CYP2C9, increasing simvastatin plasma concentrations and risk of myopathy/rhabdomyolysis.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Do not exceed simvastatin 20 mg/day when combined with amiodarone. Consider switching to pravastatin or rosuvastatin (not CYP3A4-dependent).

interactions.mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2, the main enzyme responsible for theophylline metabolism, leading to theophylline toxicity (seizures, arrhythmias).
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Reduce theophylline dose by ~30–50% on starting ciprofloxacin. Monitor serum theophylline levels closely.

interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits P-glycoprotein and reduces renal/non-renal digoxin clearance, increasing digoxin levels by ~70–100%.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Reduce digoxin dose by 50% when starting amiodarone. Monitor serum digoxin levels and ECG for bradycardia and AV block.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal tubular secretion of methotrexate and may reduce renal blood flow, leading to methotrexate accumulation and severe toxicity (mucositis, myelosuppression, nephrotoxicity).
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid concurrent use especially at high methotrexate doses. If NSAID is essential, hydrate adequately and monitor closely for methotrexate toxicity.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce prostaglandin-mediated renal blood flow, decreasing lithium renal clearance and raising plasma lithium levels by 10–50%.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid concurrent use. If NSAID is necessary, monitor serum lithium levels closely and watch for signs of toxicity (tremor, confusion, polyuria).

interactions.mechanism: Both serotonergic agents. Concurrent use raises synaptic serotonin to dangerous levels (serotonin syndrome). Fluoxetine also inhibits CYP2D6, reducing tramadol conversion to its active analgesic metabolite while increasing norepinephrine reuptake inhibition.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid. If opioid analgesia is required, use an agent with no serotonergic activity (e.g., morphine). If combination is unavoidable, monitor for agitation, hyperthermia, clonus, and hyperreflexia.

interactions.mechanism: Life-threatening serotonin syndrome. MAOIs irreversibly inhibit MAO-A and MAO-B; SSRIs block serotonin reuptake, causing profound serotonergic excess.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Absolutely contraindicated. Allow ≥14 days washout after stopping an MAOI before starting an SSRI. Allow ≥5 weeks after stopping fluoxetine (long half-life) before starting an MAOI.

interactions.mechanism: Sildenafil inhibits PDE-5, potentiating cGMP-mediated vasodilation. Nitrates generate nitric oxide activating guanylate cyclase. Combined effect causes profound hypotension, potentially fatal.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Absolutely contraindicated. Do not use any PDE-5 inhibitor within 24 h of a short-acting nitrate or 48 h of a long-acting nitrate.

interactions.mechanism: Dual renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS) blockade increases risk of hyperkalaemia, acute kidney injury, and hypotension without additional cardiovascular benefit in most patients.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid combination except in specific specialist-supervised heart failure regimens. Monitor renal function and potassium if used together.

interactions.mechanism: Metronidazole inhibits acetaldehyde dehydrogenase causing disulfiram-like reaction: flushing, nausea, vomiting, tachycardia, and hypotension.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Advise complete alcohol avoidance during metronidazole therapy and for 48 hours after the last dose.

interactions.mechanism: Rifampicin is a potent inducer of CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein, dramatically reducing plasma concentrations of ethinylestradiol and progestins, leading to contraceptive failure.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Use additional non-hormonal contraception during rifampicin therapy and for 4 weeks after stopping. Consider depot medroxyprogesterone or an IUD.

interactions.mechanism: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, reducing conversion of clopidogrel to its active thiol metabolite, potentially reducing antiplatelet effect by ~40–50%.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Prefer pantoprazole (weaker CYP2C19 inhibitor) if a PPI is necessary. The clinical significance remains debated, but avoid omeprazole in high-risk patients (e.g., stent thrombosis).

interactions.mechanism: Alcohol inhibits hepatic gluconeogenesis and can cause hypoglycaemia in diabetic patients. Chronic heavy drinking increases metformin-associated lactic acidosis risk by impairing lactate clearance.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Advise moderation or avoidance of alcohol. Assess hepatic function periodically. Hold metformin if binge drinking occurs.

interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin is a potent CYP3A4 inhibitor. Atorvastatin is predominantly metabolised by CYP3A4. Co-administration can increase atorvastatin AUC by up to 56%, raising risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Temporarily suspend atorvastatin during clarithromycin courses. Use azithromycin (does not inhibit CYP3A4) as an antibiotic alternative when possible.

interactions.mechanism: Divalent cations (Al³⁺, Mg²⁺, Ca²⁺) in antacids chelate ciprofloxacin in the GI tract, reducing oral bioavailability by up to 90%.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Administer ciprofloxacin at least 2 hours before or 6 hours after antacids, iron supplements, or calcium-containing products.

interactions.mechanism: Additive negative chronotropic and negative dromotropic effects on the SA and AV nodes. Both drugs slow AV conduction; combination may cause severe bradycardia or complete heart block.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid concurrent use. If essential, start at lowest doses with continuous cardiac monitoring. Use dihydropyridine calcium channel blockers (e.g., amlodipine) instead of verapamil or diltiazem with beta-blockers.

interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibitors reduce aldosterone secretion via RAAS blockade, impairing potassium excretion. Supplemental potassium risks hyperkalaemia, with potential for life-threatening arrhythmias.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Monitor serum potassium and renal function regularly. Avoid routine potassium supplementation unless clearly indicated (documented hypokalaemia).

interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone and its active metabolite desethylamiodarone inhibit CYP2C9 (main S-warfarin metaboliser) and CYP3A4, increasing warfarin levels. Effect may persist for months after amiodarone discontinuation.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose by 30–50% when starting amiodarone. Monitor INR weekly during initiation, then monthly. Note: the interaction persists for weeks to months after stopping amiodarone.

interactions.mechanism: Amlodipine is a moderate inhibitor of CYP3A4, raising simvastatin plasma levels (AUC increased ~77%), increasing myopathy risk.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Do not exceed simvastatin 20 mg/day when combined with amlodipine. Consider rosuvastatin or pravastatin as safer alternatives.
